channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Louisiana lawmakers pass income and corporate tax cuts, raising statewide sales tax to pay for it
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana’s GOP-dominated legislature passed tax cuts on personal and corporate income on Friday in exchange for a statewide sales tax increase, giving Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Storm inundates Northern California with rain, heavy snow. Thousands remain in the dark in Seattle
item.description: HEALDSBURG, Calif. (AP) — Heavy downpours fell over much of Northern California on Friday, causing small landslides, overflowing a river and flooding some streets, including in parts of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: NOAH BERGER and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colorado funeral home owners who let nearly 190 bodies decay plead guilty to corpse abuse
item.description: COLORADO SPRINGS, Colo. (AP) — The owners of a Colorado funeral home who let nearly 190 bodies decay in a room-temperature building and gave grieving families fake ashes pleaded guilty on Friday to corpse abuse.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump expected to name investor Scott Bessent as Treasury secretary pick
item.description: Bessent, founder and CEO of hedge fund Key Square Group, served as an economic adviser to the Trump campaign, often appearing on financial talk shows to argue in favor of the president-elect's economic policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Scott Bessent, Trump's pick for treasury secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has chosen money manager Scott Bessent, an advocate for deficit reduction and deregulation, to serve as his next treasury secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: McDonald's unveils new value menu, extends $5 Meal Deal
item.description: McDonald’s is offering its customers more ways to save on Big Macs, McNuggets, and other food items beginning in the New Year. The fast-food giant announced on Friday that it will launch a new everyday value menu on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Woman in coma after trying to save pro golfer's 6-year-old son from drowning
item.description: Professional golfer Brian Harman revealed that a close family friend has been in a coma for weeks after trying to save his 6-year-old son from a near-drowning.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, Fox 5

item.title: Texas approves optional Bible-based lessons for public schools
item.description: Texas gave final approval Friday to optional biblical lesson plans for kindergarten through 5th grade classes in state public schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No ball at Tropicana Field in 2026 after Milton; new ballpark deal dies: Tampa Bay Rays
item.description: The St. Petersburg City Council has voted to spend over $23 million to repair Tropicana Field after Hurricane Milton destroyed the roof on the home of the Tampa Bay Rays.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, Fox 5

item.title: 3 Utah children missing since 2022 found living with FLDS Church, returned to mother
item.description: Three Utah children missing for two years have been located living with members of the Fundamentalist Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, according to the Fredonia Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, WGN 9

item.title: 2 convicted in human smuggling case after Indian family froze to death on US-Canada border
item.description: A jury has convicted two men of charges related to human smuggling for their roles in an international operation that led to the deaths of a family of Indian migrants who froze while trying to cross the Canada-U.S. border during a 2022 blizzard.
item.dc:creator: Mark Vancleave And Michael Goldberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: President of Atlanta's historically Black Spelman College steps down after leave of absence
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Spelman College's president won't be coming back from her leave of absence and is stepping down permanently, the historically Black women's college announced Thursday. The college, which has more than 3,000 students, hasn't said why Dr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: State of the Rio Grande: Officials discuss new sources of water from Mexico
item.description: Texas and Mexican officials held a seminar Thursday to hash out the current and future State of the Rio Grande as waters in the international river continue to dwindle, threatening border communities.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Advocates fear national book ban under unified GOP government
item.description: A Republican trifecta in Washington is raising concerns over the potential for national book ban legislation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: New York judge calls off Trump hush money sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s sentencing in his New York criminal case will not go forward as planned next week as his attorneys push to dismiss the prosecution following his election victory. Judge Juan Merchan, who oversees the trial proceedings, announced the adjournment in a letter Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Clonazepam tablets recalled over 'life-threatening' label mistake
item.description: The company that produces the tablets has said the medication is mislabeled with the incorrect strength on the carton.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Emperor penguin who swam to Australia released 20 days after waddling onto beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia was released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Family of woman who went missing on cross-country vacation says cryptic texts sent from phone unlike her
item.description: Hannah Kobayashi never got on her flight from LA to NYC on Nov. 8 and disappeared in Los Angeles; new surveillance footage shows her with a mysterious man, her family says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Perez Hilton says Jussie Smollett decision won't help 'tanked' career: Court didn't dispute hate crime hoax
item.description: Pop culture expert and crime buff Perez Hilton weighs in on the Illinois Supreme Court's decision to toss the hate crime hoax conviction of Jussie Smollett.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KRON4

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: What's next for Matt Gaetz: 5 possibilities
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz’s (R-Fla.) political future is up in the air following his announcement that he is taking his name out of the running to be attorney general in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats will push for release of Gaetz report despite withdrawal from AG running
item.description: Rep. Sean Casten (D-Ill.), who had introduced a resolution on releasing the report, said the public still has the right to know.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Number of women who are state lawmakers inches up to a record high
item.description: Women will for the first time make up a majority of state legislators in Colorado and New Mexico next year, but at least 13 states saw losses in female representation after the November election, according to a count released Thursday by the Rutgers Center for American Women and Politics.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Carolina official overseeing hurricane rebuilding efforts is no longer in role
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The top official for North Carolina's efforts to rebuild communities after natural disasters is no longer working in the position just a few days after a scathing rebuke of her work from GOP state lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gaetz withdraws from attorney general consideration
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) has withdrawn his nomination to be Attorney General for President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KTLA 5

item.title: Gaetz withdraws as Trump's pick for attorney general
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) -- Matt Gaetz withdrew Thursday as President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general following continued scrutiny over a federal sex trafficking investigation that cast doubt on his ability to be confirmed as the nation's chief federal law enforcement officer.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: DOJ asks judge to order Google to sell Chrome
item.description: The DOJ’s proposal would require Google to divest from Chrome and prohibit the search giant from owning another browser for five years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Students can now apply for federal college aid. Officials hope for a smoother rollout this year
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal FAFSA form used to apply for college financial aid opened to high school seniors nationwide on Thursday, a year after a disastrous rollout by the Biden administration delayed the application for months.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US charges one of the world's richest people with fraud
item.description: Indian businessman Gautam Adani, one of the world's richest people, was charged by the U.S. on allegations of fraud.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The FBI arrested a man who's been charged with planning an attack on the New York Stock Exchange
item.description: A Florida man was arrested Wednesday and charged with a plot to “reboot” the U.S. government by planting a bomb at the New York Stock Exchange this week and detonating it with a remote-controlled device, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4
